cards.grammarNote: 일하다 (to work) → 일하 + 여야 해요 → 일해야 해요. 하다 verbs contract: 하여야 → 해야. 오늘 = today.

cards.front: I need to practice every day.
cards.grammarNote: 연습하다 (to practice) → 연습해야 해요. 매일 = every day. Common in hobby and skill contexts.

cards.front: I have to submit the report.
cards.grammarNote: 제출하다 (to submit) → 제출해야 해요. 보고서 = report. Professional context for 해야 해요.
배우다 (to learn) → stem 배우 ends in 우 (vowel, neither ㅏ nor ㅗ) → 배워야 해요 (배우 + 어야 → 배워야). 기술 = skill.

cards.front: I have to attend the meeting.
cards.grammarNote: 참석하다 (to attend) → 참석해야 해요. 회의 = meeting. Very common in work-context sentences.
모으다 (to save/collect) → stem 모으, ㅡ drops before 아/어 → 모아야 해요. 돈 = money.
지키다 (to keep/observe) → stem 지키 + 어야 해요 → 지켜야 해요 (contracted). 시간을 지키다 = to be on time / keep time.

cards.front: I have to prepare a presentation.
cards.grammarNote: 준비하다 (to prepare) → 준비해야 해요. 발표 = presentation/announcement.

cards.front: I must focus on my hobby.
cards.grammarNote: 집중하다 (to focus/concentrate) → 집중해야 해요. 취미 = hobby.

cards.front: I have to finish it by Friday.
cards.grammarNote: 끝내다 (to finish) → stem 끝내 + 어야 해요 → 끝내야 해요. 까지 = by/until. 금요일 = Friday.

cards.front: You have to keep practicing to improve.
cards.grammarNote: 키우다 (to grow/develop) + 려면 = if you want to... 계속 = continuously. A motivational sentence combining -려면 and -해야 해요.
